title: CFPB alum Chopra to lead state consumer agency
description: Former CFPB head Rohit Chopra will lead a new California agency with oversight of consumer affairs and financial protection, among other duties.

title: The fight against fraud revs up
description: Maybe crusaders against financial fraud and scams can finally make some headway with help from Congress, the White House and Nacha.

title: Fiserv seeks better days ahead
description: The payments processor and bank tech provider sought Thursday to bolster confidence in the company’s plan for a recovery.

title: BNPL giants battle at Walmart
description: The retail behemoth swapped buy now, pay later provider Affirm for Klarna last year, but both BNPL companies say Walmart business is good.

title: Dive Deposits: Affirm’s AI stance runs counter to trend
description: While some fintechs are content to do more with less through AI, BNPL platform Affirm simply aims to do more, as its top execs eschew human layoffs in favor of artificial intelligence.

title: Block inches closer to DOJ settlement
description: The company set aside $240 million in the first quarter as it negotiates with the Justice Department over allegations in a 2023 short seller’s report.

title: Shopify loses bid to toss Sezzle lawsuit
description: The e-commerce platform can't jettison plausible monopoly claims made by the buy now, pay later company, a federal judge ruled Monday.

title: PayPal settles with DOJ over DEI
description: The digital payments company agreed Tuesday to a settlement with the Department of Justice over a 2020 diversity investment fund.

title: FIS discounts Pismo competition
description: The payments processor and core tech provider doesn’t expect competition from that Visa business to make an impact.

title: Judge combines Fiserv lawsuits
description: Shareholder complaints against the payment processor are closely related and should be adjudicated in a New York case, a Wisconsin federal judge ruled.

title: Reinventing finance and tax: Leaders see AI and e-invoicing as agents of change
description: How global teams are scaling smarter — and where risks remain.

title: Automation’s promise: Distinguishing what delivers and works in the agentic age
description: Finance automation promised to fix the close. It didn't. Here's what actually changes things.

title: From oversight to ownership: The CFO’s new role in AI
description: The CFO's role in AI has shifted from oversight to ownership. Here's what that means.

title: Share of women on company boards falls below 30%
description: The findings of a study conducted by Women 50/50 echo other recent research revealing persistent limits on opportunities for women at the highest levels of business.

title: Timberwolves, Lynx tap former Penguins exec for CFO, COO
description: The change comes after the $1.5 billion sale of the teams in 2025 and the subsequent appointment of a new CEO.

title: What puts organizations at risk of losing AI talent?
description: “AI adoption is a culture issue, not just a training issue,” a Gartner report said.

title: AICPA seeks IRS relief on newly expanded excise tax
description: Without regulatory exceptions, some nonprofits could be forced to restructure their workforce or even close, the AICPA warned.

title: Cisco to cut nearly 4,000 jobs as AI shift accelerates
description: “Things are moving incredibly fast right now,” Cisco’s CFO said, as the company sharpens its focus on AI.

title: Retail sales slow to 0.5% growth as inflation erodes affordability
description: Wages rose at annual rate of just 3.6% in April, lagging inflation for the first time in three years and weakening household spending power.

title: FASB tackles transferable tax credits
description: Accountants have clamored for guidance to address a growing number of tax credits offered to incentivize investments in energy and other projects.

title: Designing digital banking experiences that win Gen Z and millennial loyalty
description: Empowering financial institutions to connect with Gen Z demands crafting an holistic experience that caters to their evolving expectations, behaviors and lifestyles.

title: FDIC: Top depositors led 2023 runs at failed regional banks
description: Silicon Valley Bank, Signature Bank and First Republic Bank each lost about half of their deposits in a matter of days in early March 2023, the regulator found.

title: Senate panel approves crypto bill
description: The Clarity Act – with stablecoin yield language intact – moved to the full Senate. Some last-minute amendments resurfaced, but an anti-money laundering measure was denied.

title: Stellantis gets FDIC’s ILC charter approval
description: The car manufacturer joins Ford and GM in receiving regulatory approvals this year to obtain an industrial loan company charter.

title: How Alaska’s Credit Union 1 serves ‘banking tundras’
description: CEO Mark Burgess discusses the challenges of serving disparate, isolated communities – from transporting teller machines by plane to using remote capabilities to capture loan signatures.

title: Senate confirms Warsh as next Fed chair
description: The mostly party-line vote comes amid concerns of whether Kevin Warsh will maintain central bank independence or hew closer to President Trump’s interest-rate demands than his predecessor.

title: New England mutual banks partner but it’s ‘not an acquisition’
description: New Hampshire-based Mascoma Bank and Maine-based Androscoggin Bank will combine their parent companies into a new mutual holding company. “Scale in banking matters,” Mascoma CEO Clay Adams said.

title: CFPB’s Vought defends terminating Citi consent order
description: “There are no unfulfilled obligations as of the date of termination, and Citi committed to ongoing monitoring regardless of termination,” the CFPB acting director wrote to lawmakers this week.

title: Dive Deposits: Affirm’s AI stance runs counter to trend
description: While some fintechs are content to do more with less through AI, BNPL platform Affirm simply aims to do more, as its top execs eschew human layoffs in favor of artificial intelligence.

title: Fintech Parker files for bankruptcy
description: “Three weeks ago, I thought Parker was going to be acquired in a deal worth nearly $90M,” the fintech’s co-founder, Yacine Sibous, wrote Saturday. “Yesterday, we filed for Chapter 7.”

title: UK regulators warn of new threats from frontier AI models
description: The FCA, Bank of England and HM Treasury are warning banks to prepare for a new wave of powerful cyber attacks from AI-driven 'frontier' models.

title: OpenAI begins roll out of personal finance suite
description: OpenAI has released a preview of a new integration with account aggregator Plaid that enables ChatGPT users to connect all of their accounts and ask questions ranging from spending analysis to future financial planning.

title: Nationwide gives abuse survivors the power to block harmful payment messages
description: Nationwide has released a new in-app features that gives abuse survivors the power to block harmful payment messages.

title: Sygnum delivers AI agent-driven digital asset transactions
description: Swiss digital asset bank Sygnum is to use AI agents to test live on-chain transactions using a 'human-in-the-loop' design, with the client signing every action and private keys never leaving their device.

title: Zopa and ClearScore sign up 22 members for GenAI upskilling push
description: Zopa Bank and ClearScore have signed up 22 members to their coalition dedicated to upskilling 100,000 fintech and banking professionals in AI disciplines by 2030.

title: 9 in 10 social media posts by finfluencers are low quality - research
description: Almost 90% of social media posts from financial influencers are low quality, according to research from Queen Mary University of London.

title: Fiserv unveils agentic AI operating system
description: Fiserv has launched AgentOS, an agentic AI operating system designed to help financial institutions deploy, manage, and scale agents across their banking workflows.

title: NAB buys A2A payments platform Banked
description: National Australia Bank has acquired account-to-account payments platform Banked. Financial terms were not disclosed.

title: US banking committee advances Clarity Act to Senate
description: On Thursday, the Republican-led Senate Banking Committee advanced the Clarity Act which would regulate digital assets.

title: nsave to offer Syrians international USD accounts
description: Offshore banking platform nsave will launch financial services for underbanked Syrians both abroad and in Syria.

title: Klarna breaks even first time after IPO listing
description: Swedish fintech firm Klarna broke even for the first time since it announced its $15 billion New York IPO last September.

title: Fasset raises $51m for stablecoin-powered digital banking
description: Stablecoin-powered digital banking and investment platform Fasset has raised $51 million in Series B funding.

title: CMC Markets launches spread-betting account for retail clients
description: CMC Markets (“CMC”), a FTSE 250 company and global leader in multi-asset online trading and investing, has launched Spectre for retail clients, following strong demand after the product was initially introduced for professional traders.

title: Zepz joins NCA efforts to identify financial signals linked to child sexual abuse
description: Zepz, the global payments group behind WorldRemit and Sendwave, today announced their participation in a National Economic Crime Centre (NECC) Public Private Partnership (PPP) Cell focused on combatting child sexual abuse (CSA).

title: PRA consults on ring-fencing reforms
description: The Prudential Regulation Authority (PRA) has today announced plans to consult on reforming rules around shared operational services for ring-fenced banks.

title: CFIT unveils Open Property roadmap
description: The Centre for Finance, Innovation and Technology (CFIT) today marks the culmination of the first phase of its Open Property Coalition, publishing a landmark Roadmap for Open Property that has the potential to transform the UK’s homebuying process - and signals the next phase of the UK’s Smart Data journey beyond Open Banking and Open Finance.

title: Digital Assets Clearing Center secures $10 million investment
description: Digital Asset Clearing Center (DACC.HK), a next-generation financial market infrastructure for the tokenized economy, today announced US$10 million in funding from strategic partners including Conflux, Transaction Technologies Limited (“TTL”) and Global InfoTech.

title: SGX FX conneccts with Chainlink
description: SGX FX, a leading technology partner for the global institutional FX ecosystem, has adopted Chainlink, the industry-standard oracle platform, to broaden access to its OTC FX data and support greater flexibility in how institutional participants consume trusted market information.

title: FCA and Bank of England set out shared vision for tokenisation in UK wholesale markets
description: UK financial firms can adopt tokenisation and distributed ledger technology (DLT) with greater confidence, as the Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) and the Bank of England set out a shared vision and seek industry views on the future of UK wholesale markets.

title: State Employees’ Credit Union signs with Corelation
description: State Employees’ Credit Union (SECU) has signed with credit union core processor Corelation, Inc., announcing plans to convert to the KeyStone core. Based in Raleigh, North Carolina, SECU is the second largest credit union in the United States with over $59 billion in assets, serving more than 2.9 million members.

title: OMS appoints Simon Tippett as head of delivery
description: One Mortgage System (OMS), the CRM and loan origination platform for intermediaries and lenders, has announced the appointment of Simon Tippett as Head of Delivery as the business continues to strengthen its technical and operational capabilities during its next phase of growth.

title: Banking Circle names Kush Saxena as CEO
description: Banking Circle Group, the financial technology platform for global commerce, today announces the appointment of Kush Saxena as Group Chief Executive Officer.

title: Aon to modernize how brokers access capital and syndicate risk
description: Aon plc (NYSE: AON), a leading global professional services firm, today announced plans to launch Aon Digital Placement Exchange (Aon DPX), a new digital trading platform designed to modernize how brokers access capital and syndicate risk.

title: Bitget Wallet hires Uber Jack Zhai as head of the Americas
description: Bitget Wallet, the everyday finance app, has appointed Jack Zhai as Head of the Americas. Zhai helped scale Uber during its aggressive international expansion.

description: “We plan to sell it to another family member for the appraised value.”

title: Three ways the AI boom may fizzle — and what happens to stocks next
description: A pullback in big tech capex may rattle markets, say Panmure Liberum strategists.

title: Here’s where Treasury yields will peak and open up a rare opportunity to buy stocks and bonds, says Wall Street veteran
description: The Treasury yield could peak near 5% in coming weeks, which will be a chance to buy stocks, says Ed Yardeni.

title: ‘He didn’t really pay attention’: I told my friend he left millions of dollars on the table in retirement. Was I right?
description: “He lived very frugally and maxed out his 401(k)s.”

title: My husband took out a $100,000 Parent PLUS loan for his daughter. She dropped out, citing mental-health issues. Should we refinance?
description: “There is little to no chance that she will ever be able to repay these loans.”

title: As these market drivers start to weaken, it’s time for investors to buy protection, says Deutsche Bank

title: ‘I’m already feeling anxious’: My ex-husband offered to give me a $30,000 lump sum for child support. Is there a catch?
description: “He has not paid the last two months, and it is not court-ordered.”

title: A ‘private celebrity compound’: Selena Gomez lists her L.A. estate for $6.5 million
description: Selena Gomez is preparing to bid farewell to her former bachelorette pad, listing the sprawling Encino home for $6.49 million, just 6 months after she married Benny Blanco.

title: Musician Sheryl Crow is selling part of her Nashville estate for $1.8 million
description: Country music legend Sheryl Crow is offloading a small slice of her extravagant Nashville estate for $1.8 million—more than a decade after adding the 2.2-acre parcel to her longtime Tennessee compound.

title: Dow futures drop, oil prices push higher as Iran war remains stuck in stalemate
description: U.S. stock-index futures fell and crude prices rose on Sunday, after the market’s rally stalled last week as oil prices rose sharply amid the impasse in the war with Iran.
